source.sourcePath: raw/sources/Project/02_CYAN/1. DHF/16. Manual/[GT series] User Manual_EN (Detector).docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Manufacturer user manual for H&abyz GT1717C/GT1717CP digital flat panel X-ray detectors covering intended use, safety, installation, maintenance, EMC, regulatory compliance, labeling, warranty, and revision history.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use to support wiki facts about product intended use, model identification, manufacturer, operating constraints, safety warnings, maintenance, EMC compliance, WEEE disposal, and revision history.
keyPoints: The GT1717C/GT1717CP Detector converts medical images into digital format using digital radiography equipment and stores and transmits the images.
The detector cannot be used for mammography.
The equipment complies with the IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standard for medical devices.
Product information identifies the product as a Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector manufactured by H&abyz Co., Ltd. in Korea.
Revision history lists Version 1 dated 2026.03.19 as revised due to overall content update.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: Product Information Product Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector Model GT1717C / GT1717CP Manufacturer H&abyz Co., Ltd.

entities.name: European Union and EEA
entities.evidence: European Union (and EEA*) only This symbol indicates that this product is not to be disposed with your household waste, according to the WEEE Directive (2012/19/EC) and your national law.

entities.name: CX Team
entities.evidence: Department H&abyz CX Team E-mail cx@abyzr.com

concepts.name: Intended Use
concepts.evidence: The GT1717C/GT1717CP Detector is a device that converts medical images into digital format using digital radiography (DR) equipment and stores and transmits the images. It also includes software.

concepts.name: Contra-Indications
concepts.evidence: This detector cannot be used for mammography.

concepts.name: Regulatory Compliance
concepts.evidence: This equipment complies with the IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standard for medical devices.

concepts.name: Electromagnetic Compatibility
concepts.evidence: This equipment has passed electromagnetic compatibility (EMC/EMI) testing; however, electromagnetic interference may occur in locations with high levels of electromagnetic activity.

concepts.name: WEEE Disposal
concepts.evidence: This product is not to be disposed with your household waste, according to the WEEE Directive (2012/19/EC) and your national law.

concepts.name: Product Lifetime
concepts.evidence: Product life is approximately 5 years under proper regular inspection and maintenance.

concepts.name: Periodic Inspection and Maintenance
concepts.evidence: To ensure the safety of patients, users, and third parties, and to maintain equipment performance and reliability, perform a periodic inspection at least once a year.

evidence.claim: The document is a user manual for GT1717C/GT1717CP detector models.
evidence.evidenceText: This user manual explains how to use the GT1717/GT1717CP detector models made by H&abyz. Use this manual to set up the GT1717/GT1717CP detector as well as understand its various functions.

evidence.claim: The source identifies the model names as GT1717C and GT1717CP.
evidence.evidenceText: Model name: GT1717C, GT1717CP

evidence.claim: The GT1717C/GT1717CP Detector converts medical images into digital format using digital radiography equipment and stores and transmits images.
evidence.evidenceText: The GT1717C/GT1717CP Detector is a device that converts medical images into digital format using digital radiography (DR) equipment and stores and transmits the images. It also includes software.

evidence.claim: The detector is used to examine patients with, or suspected of having, musculoskeletal injuries and respiratory diseases.
evidence.evidenceText: The GT1717C/GT1717CP Detector is used to examine patients with, or suspected of having, musculoskeletal injuries and respiratory diseases.

evidence.claim: The detector cannot be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: This detector cannot be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: Only a physician or legally certified operator should use the product.
evidence.evidenceText: Only a physician or a legally certified operator should use this product.

evidence.claim: GT1717C has standard 300 micrometer CsI deposition thickness and GT1717CP has premium 500 micrometer CsI deposition thickness.
evidence.evidenceText: Model Name GT1717C GT1717CP CsI Deposition Thickness Standard (300μm) Premium (500μm)

evidence.claim: The detector sensor type is PIN-TFT using a-Si TFT.
evidence.evidenceText: Sensor Type PIN-TFT (a-Si, TFT)

evidence.claim: The detector dimensions are 460 x 476 x 15.8 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Dimensions (H x W x D) 460 x 476 x 15.8 mm

evidence.claim: The GT1717C weighs 3.95 kg and the GT1717CP weighs 4.2 kg.
evidence.evidenceText: Weight 3.95kg (GT1717C) / 4.2kg (GT1717CP)

evidence.claim: The detector uses CsI scintillator and has a 3072 x 3072 pixel effective array.
evidence.evidenceText: Scintillator CsI Effective Array 3072 x 3072 pixels

evidence.claim: The detector supports AED and Line Trigger acquisition modes.
evidence.evidenceText: Trigger Mode AED (Automatic Exposure Detection) Line Trigger (Manual)

evidence.claim: The product supports image transfer over 1 Gigabit Ethernet via Power over Ethernet.
evidence.evidenceText: Image Transfer 1 Giga-bit Ethernet via PoE (Power over Ethernet)

evidence.claim: The detector operating environment is 5 to 40 degrees Celsius, 15 to 90% humidity, and 700 to 1060 hPa pressure.
evidence.evidenceText: Use Environment Item Operation Temperature 5~40℃ Humidity 15~90% H.R. Pressure 700~1060hPa

evidence.claim: The detector storage and transportation environment is -15 to 55 degrees Celsius, 10 to 95% humidity, and 500 to 1060 hPa pressure.
evidence.evidenceText: Use Environment Item Storage & Transportation Temperature -15~55℃ Humidity 10~95% H.R. Pressure 500~1060hPa

evidence.claim: The detector uniform load limit is 150 kgf for 60 seconds and partial load limit is 100 kgf over a 40 mm active area for 10 seconds.
evidence.evidenceText: Detector load limit Uniform load Max 150kgf (60 Sec.) Partial load Max 100kgf, φ40mm Active Area (10 Sec.)

evidence.claim: The equipment complies with IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standard for medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: This equipment complies with the IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standard for medical devices.

evidence.claim: The detector has passed EMC/EMI testing, but electromagnetic interference may occur in high electromagnetic activity locations.
evidence.evidenceText: This equipment has passed electromagnetic compatibility (EMC/EMI) testing; however, electromagnetic interference may occur in locations with high levels of electromagnetic activity.

evidence.claim: The primary function of the GT1717C/GT1717CP detector is acquiring diagnostic images and transmitting them to a PC workstation.
evidence.evidenceText: The primary function of the GT1717C/GT1717CP detector is to acquire diagnostic images and transmit them to a PC (Workstation).

evidence.claim: RF emissions compliance is CISPR 11 Group 1 Class A.
evidence.evidenceText: RF Emissions CISPR 11 Group 1 RF Emissions CISPR 11 Class A

evidence.claim: Harmonics emissions compliance is IEC 61000-3-2 Class A.
evidence.evidenceText: Harmonics Emissions IEC 61000-3-2 Class A

evidence.claim: Voltage fluctuations and flicker emissions compliance is IEC 61000-3-3.
evidence.evidenceText: Voltage fluctuations/flicker emissions IEC 61000-3-3 Complies

evidence.claim: The product should not be disposed with household waste in the European Union and EEA under the WEEE Directive 2012/19/EC and national law.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union (and EEA*) only This symbol indicates that this product is not to be disposed with your household waste, according to the WEEE Directive (2012/19/EC) and your national law.

evidence.claim: Product life is approximately 5 years with proper regular inspection and maintenance.
evidence.evidenceText: Product life is approximately 5 years under proper regular inspection and maintenance.

evidence.claim: Periodic inspection should be performed at least once a year.
evidence.evidenceText: To ensure the safety of patients, users, and third parties, and to maintain equipment performance and reliability, perform a periodic inspection at least once a year.

evidence.claim: Calibration must be performed by a qualified service engineer.
evidence.evidenceText: Calibration must be performed by a qualified service engineer.

evidence.claim: Product information identifies the product as a Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector manufactured by H&abyz Co., Ltd.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Information Product Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector Model GT1717C / GT1717CP Manufacturer H&abyz Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: The country of origin is Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Country of Origin Made in Korea

reviewFlags.reason: No separate publication date or effective date is provided; documentDate is taken from the Version 1 revision history date.
Authority signal is general and the source is a manufacturer user manual; no issuing regulatory authority is identified beyond manufacturer and jurisdictional references.
Potential OCR or source quality issues are present, including spelling anomalies such as Dimention, Metho, Mislight, and inconsistent H&abyz/H&Abyz capitalization.
Conflicting wording appears in EMC sections: the product is described as a wired detector, but several EMC passages refer to this Wireless Detector.
One manual sentence refers to GT1717/GT1717CP detector models, while source metadata and product information identify GT1717C/GT1717CP.
